Scripts

Screenwriting Basics 101
Screenwriting 101

The screenplay is the
backbone of filmmaking.

Every scene, every line of dialogue, every beat — it all lives on the page first. Here are the six core elements of the Master Scene format, and exactly where each one lives in WroteNote.

📄 1 page ≈ 1 minute
🎬 90–120 pages = feature
✍️ 6 elements to master
Live preview — all 6 elements
01
Scene Heading (Slug Line)

The slug line opens every scene — it tells us where and when. Always in ALL CAPS on its own line.

  • INT. = inside  /  EXT. = outside  /  INT./EXT. = both
  • Location — keep it short
  • Time — DAY, NIGHT, or CONTINUOUS / MOMENTS LATER
In WroteNote: Click SCENE in the toolbar. WroteNote auto-numbers your scenes.
Demo
02
Action Lines

Describes everything visible on screen. Always in present tense. Never describe thoughts or feelings that can't be filmed.

  • A character's FIRST APPEARANCE is in ALL CAPS
  • Key SOUND EFFECTS and props can be capped
  • Never write camera directions — that's the director's job
In WroteNote: Action is the default block. Press Enter after a slug line.
Demo
03
Character Cue & Dialogue

The character's name in ALL CAPS introduces any dialogue. Parentheticals offer brief performance notes — use them sparingly.

  • Add (V.O.) = voiceover  /  (O.S.) = off-screen
  • Parentheticals go under the name or mid-dialogue
  • Trust your actors — don't over-direct
In WroteNote: Click CHAR or press Tab. WroteNote colour-codes each character.
Demo
04
Transitions

Holdovers from the studio era. Right-aligned with a colon. Use sparingly — editorial decisions belong to editors.

  • CUT TO:   DISSOLVE TO:   SMASH CUT TO:
  • FADE IN: opens a script  /  FADE OUT. closes it
  • Only use when crucial to the story
In WroteNote: Click TRANS — it auto-aligns right.
Demo
The Golden Rule
1 page = 1 minute of screen time

The entire format — margins, Courier 12pt, block widths — was engineered so one page equals roughly one minute. This lets producers estimate length and budget before a single frame is shot.

90
pages min · ~1.5 hr
110
sweet spot · ~1h 50m
120
pages max · ~2 hr
Ready to write your first scene?

WroteNote handles all the formatting automatically.

Typography
Interface Font

Color Palette

The primary color used for highlights, buttons, and active states.

Sets the gradient used in script blocks, banners, and highlights.

Account & Security
Display Name
Change Password
Format: 1 letter + 4 digits (e.g. A1234)
Security Question
Custom Dictionary
Words added to your dictionary won't be flagged by spellcheck in any editor.
Data & Storage
Scripts
All scripts are saved to this browser's localStorage under the key wrotenote_scripts. Data lives in this browser only — it does not sync across devices or browsers.
Exports
Exported files are downloaded to your browser's default Downloads folder:
• .wrotenote — full script JSON (Scripts panel)
• .txt — plain text export (Scripts panel)
• .wn-breakdown — breakdown session JSON (Breakdowns panel)
• .ttx / .pdf — Final Draft XML & browser print PDF (Breakdowns panel)
Backup & Restore
Export all your WroteNote data — scripts, docs, sheets, kanban boards, and settings — into a single backup file. Restore it any time to recover everything.
Preferences
UI settings (theme, density, page numbers, page splitters) are saved to localStorage under keys prefixed wn_.

Format compatibility
.wrotenote
Perfect — full fidelity, no data loss
.fountain .fdx
Excellent — structure fully preserved
.txt
Good — auto-detected from formatting
.rtf .docx
Fair — formatting may be lost
.pdf
Limited — requires internet · may vary
Export as .fountain from WritersDuet, Highland, Arc Studio, or Final Draft for best results.
